intended_rule: Remove residual leveraged and weak historical positions when cleanup triggers confirm, then evaluate effectiveness by rule compliance and avoided downside exposure.
actual_execution: SOXL sold 2 at 239.34 for 478.68 USD, UGL sold 10 at 52.385 for 523.85 USD, and INTC sold 10 at 104.75 for 1,047.50 USD. Latest cleanup cash impact was about 2,050.03 USD before fees.
account_risk_improvement: SOXL, UGL, and INTC exposure was reduced to zero; cash/cash-equivalent increased materially; remaining risk is concentrated in core positions and GGLL.
short_term_price_outcome: Subsequent prices were SOXL around 176.98-182.54, UGL around 51.18-51.38, and INTC around 96.90-99.17. These moves support effectiveness but do not define correctness.
review_conclusion: Cleanup was highly effective and rule-compliant. The portfolio can transition from historical_risk_cleanup_mode to core_position_defense_mode.
lessons_learned: Cleanup Effectiveness Review should store symbol, cleanup execution price, subsequent market price, avoided exposure estimate, cleanup effectiveness score, rule compliance score, and account structure impact.
Latest cleanup cycle cash impact was approximately 2,050.03 USD before fees.
Total SOXL cleanup cash impact was approximately 1,222.68 USD before fees across both SOXL fills.
Execution correctness is based on rule compliance and risk reduction, not best possible exit price.
next_rule_update: Retire SOXL, UGL, and INTC active cleanup rules; activate core-position defense rules for NVDA, GGLL, and GLD.
